packet.name: B (Chicago A, Harvard A, Sheffield, Illinois A)

bonuses.leadin: In one opera, the oboe occasionally interjects as a character performing this action laments her withered flowers and sings the aria “Ah! non credea mirarti / sì presto estinto, o fiore.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this title action of an 1831 opera. Elvino calls off a wedding after mistakenly believing that a woman performing this action is having an affair with Rodolfo when she ends up in his room.
A line from Amina’s “flower aria” from La Sonnambula is inscribed on the tomb of this Italian composer of bel canto operas such as Norma.

Besides Amina, Maria Callas’s signature Bellini roles include Imogene, a woman who is seduced by one of these people named Gualtiero. In an operetta, one of these title characters sings “Oh, is there not one maiden breast?”
answers: sleepwalking [or somnambulism or noctambulism; accept La Sonnambula or The Sleepwalker; prompt on sleeping; prompt on walking]
pirates [accept Il pirata or The Pirates of Penzance; accept swashbucklers or privateers or buccaneers or corsairs; prompt on sailors or robbers or thieves or thief or seamen or mariners]
answers_sanitized: sleepwalking [or somnambulism or noctambulism; accept La Sonnambula or The Sleepwalker; prompt on sleeping; prompt on walking]
Vincenzo ("veen-CHENT-so") Bellini [or Vincenzo Salvatore Carmelo Francesco Bellini]
pirates [accept Il pirata or The Pirates of Penzance; accept swashbucklers or privateers or buccaneers or corsairs; prompt on sailors or robbers or thieves or thief or seamen or mariners]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In one opera, the oboe occasionally interjects as a character performing this action laments her withered flowers and sings the aria "Ah! non credea mirarti / si presto estinto, o fiore." For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this title action of an 1831 opera. Elvino calls off a wedding after mistakenly believing that a woman performing this action is having an affair with Rodolfo when she ends up in his room.
A line from Amina's "flower aria" from La Sonnambula is inscribed on the tomb of this Italian composer of bel canto operas such as Norma.

Besides Amina, Maria Callas's signature Bellini roles include Imogene, a woman who is seduced by one of these people named Gualtiero. In an operetta, one of these title characters sings "Oh, is there not one maiden breast?"

bonuses.leadin: A 1955 La Scala production of this opera, conducted by Leonard Bernstein, is sometimes credited with purging the excessively flashy soprano antics that had become traditional earlier in the century. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera by Vincenzo (“veen-CHEN-tso”) Bellini, whose title character, Amina (“ah-MEE-nuh”), sings the aria “Ah! non credea mirarti” (“ah nawn kray-DAY-uh mee-RAHR-tee”) after being rejected by Elvino, when she can’t explain why she’s in Rodolfo’s room.
That production was headlined by this tempestuous Greek bel canto soprano, nicknamed La Divina.

The production was directed by this man, whose other collaborations with Callas at La Scala in the 1950s include productions of La vestale(“lah vess-TAHL”) and Iphigénie en Tauride(“ee-fee-zheh-NEE awn taw-REED”). He also directed a controversial 1966 production of Falstaff starring Dietrich Fischer-Dieskau (“fisher-DEESS-cow”).
answers: Luchino Visconti [or Luchino Visconti di Modrone; or Count of Lonate Pozzolo]
answers_sanitized: Luchino Visconti [or Luchino Visconti di Modrone; or Count of Lonate Pozzolo]

That production was headlined by this tempestuous Greek bel canto soprano, nicknamed La Divina.

The production was directed by this man, whose other collaborations with Callas at La Scala in the 1950s include productions of La vestale("lah vess-TAHL") and Iphigenie en Tauride("ee-fee-zheh-NEE awn taw-REED"). He also directed a controversial 1966 production of Falstaff starring Dietrich Fischer-Dieskau ("fisher-DEESS-cow").

bonuses.leadin: The soprano aria ‘Ah! non credea mirarti’ is sung by Amina in the last act of this opera as she crosses an unsafe bridge holding some withered flowers. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera in which the jealous Elviro’s repeated suspicions of Amina’s faithfulness are encouraged by the inn-owning Lisa, who wants to marry him.
The first lines of that aria from La sonnambula, which lament the early blooming of a flower, are written on the tomb of this bel canto composer of I puritani and Norma.

Unlike Bellini’s success at a young age, Donizetti wrote 30 operas before having success with this tragic opera, whose title character is still in love with Lord Richard Percy while her husband plots to replace her with her lady-in-waiting.

Unlike Bellini's success at a young age, Donizetti wrote 30 operas before having success with this tragic opera, whose title character is still in love with Lord Richard Percy while her husband plots to replace her with her lady-in-waiting.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about crazy productions of various operas, for 10 points each.
parts: A 2001 English National Opera production of an opera by this composer featured drinking, drugs, and urinating on stage. The director accused opera critics of being "weird" and "prissy" after the audience booed that production of this composer's Don Giovanni.
A 1949 production of this Richard (REE-shot) Strauss opera directed by Peter Brook featured designs by Salvador Dalí and enraged audiences. This opera includes the "Dance of the Seven Veils."

American audiences booed a 2009 production of this opera that moved the setting from the Alps to New York. In this opera, the title character Amina is thought by villagers to be both a phantom and also the lover of Count Rodolfo.
answers_sanitized: La sonnambula [or The Sleepwalker] (by Vincenzo Bellini)
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about crazy productions of various operas, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: A 2001 English National Opera production of an opera by this composer featured drinking, drugs, and urinating on stage. The director accused opera critics of being "weird" and "prissy" after the audience booed that production of this composer's Don Giovanni.
A 1949 production of this Richard (REE-shot) Strauss opera directed by Peter Brook featured designs by Salvador Dali and enraged audiences. This opera includes the "Dance of the Seven Veils."

American audiences booed a 2009 production of this opera that moved the setting from the Alps to New York. In this opera, the title character Amina is thought by villagers to be both a phantom and also the lover of Count Rodolfo.

bonuses.leadin: In 1949, this soprano shocked the opera world by singing Elvira in I Puritani just days after performing as Brunnhilde in Die Walkure. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Greek-American soprano known for her bel canto roles, wide vocal range, and dramatic intensity. She had a long rivalry with Renalda Tebaldi, and lost nearly eighty pounds during the early 1950s.
Callas was renowned for performing as Amina, the title character of this Vincenzo Bellini opera. It includes the aria “Ah! non credea,” in which Amina sings about her love for Elvino.

This Australian coloratura soprano was also known for her bel canto repertoire, and was nicknamed “La Stupenda” after singing the title role of Handel’s Alcina at Le Fenice. In 1959, she became famous for performing the title role of Zeffirelli’s production of Lucia di Lammermoor.

Callas was renowned for performing as Amina, the title character of this Vincenzo Bellini opera. It includes the aria "Ah! non credea," in which Amina sings about her love for Elvino.

This Australian coloratura soprano was also known for her bel canto repertoire, and was nicknamed "La Stupenda" after singing the title role of Handel's Alcina at Le Fenice. In 1959, she became famous for performing the title role of Zeffirelli's production of Lucia di Lammermoor.
